title: First Five Rule Instances — foundation arc capstone
description: Lands the first five rule instances at schemas/_instances/rules/ — populating the canonical governance pipeline established by rule.schema.json v1.1.0. Five instances exercise every axis of the schema: both lifecycleStatus values, all three runtime tiers in activeTier, both terminal targets in terminalTier, evidenceClass on the formalized rule's backfilled transition, gatingBinding on CI-axis rules, and cross-rule semantic relations. Closes the four-deliverable foundation arc (D-0 refs, D-1 deliverable schema, D-2 rule schema v1.1.0, D-3 instances).

outputs.description: RULE-PIPELINE-PROJECTION-CONTRACT-1 — token/information discipline: no file body crosses dispatch→chat transport boundary. Source: pipeline-quality session (N-1 of foundation arc). lifecycleStatus working-model, terminalTier automated with ciSurface PENDING (F5 fusion FU).
acceptanceCriteria: gateActions.automated.ciSurface explicitly declares PENDING with FU reference

outputs.description: RULE-PIPELINE-AMENDMENT-SUBSTRATE-RECHECK-1 — at deliverable amendment time, check scope_files overlap against other deliverables on same base branch. Source: F7 prior session (PR #261 conflict). lifecycleStatus working-model, activeTier human-loop, terminalTier automated.
acceptanceCriteria: transitions array includes working-model → human-loop with evidenceClass citing PR #261

outputs.description: RULE-CI-UNSTABLE-GATES-IDENTICALLY-RED-1 — CI mergeStateStatus UNSTABLE gates identically to red. Source: EB-agent #1 (PR #48 + #269 repeat incidents). lifecycleStatus working-model, activeTier human-loop, terminalTier automated.
acceptanceCriteria: gatingBinding declares branch pattern and CI surface
transitions includes evidenceClass citing PR #48 and PR #269

outputs.description: RULE-CI-DOCUMENTED-OVERRIDE-NOT-COMPLIANCE-1 — audit/paper-trail fields recording a policy violation do not transform the violation into compliance. Source: EB-agent #2 (TRUTH-DELIVERED override proposal refused). lifecycleStatus working-model, activeTier working-model, terminalTier automated via schema constraint.

RULE-PIPELINE-SPIKE-SUPERSEDES-NOVEL-PATTERN-1 — when execution surfaces substrate beyond declared scope, use SPIKE primitive (memory #17), not novel pattern authoring. Source: EB-agent #3. lifecycleStatus formalized (memory #17 precise), activeTier human-loop, terminalTier human-loop with automationPath null-by-nature. Proves schema's terminal-by-nature property.
acceptanceCriteria: gateActions.human-loop.automationPath explicitly declares null by nature
transitions include backfilled working-model → formalized with evidenceClass citing memory #17 and multi-session proof

outputs.description: Prospective deliverable spec at schemas/_instances/deliverables/d-2026-06-01-rule-pipeline-first-instances.json — S4 authored before any rule instance lands; S5/S6 across dispatch; S7/S8 post-merge amendment.
refs rebuild --check exit 0 verification — proves _instances/** exclusion works for rules namespace (no schema-refs.json changes despite five new instance files added).
acceptanceCriteria: refs rebuild --check exit 0 after all five rule instances land
Zero entries with file path matching _instances/rules/*

preMerge: ajv-cli@5 validate -s schemas/rule.schema.json -d schemas/_instances/rules/*.json succeeds for all 5
refs rebuild --check exit 0 after instances land
meta-schema-check.yml passes for all instance files
postMerge: main shows 5 instance files at schemas/_instances/rules/
schema-refs.json on main unchanged (no rule instance leakage)
luhtech-schema refs rebuild --check exit 0 on main

transitions.trigger: Dispatch authored after D-2 close; rule.schema.json v1.1.0 live on main (8a24cfd); five instance candidates locked from prior session + EB-agent contribution

notes: Fourth and final deliverable in the rule-pipeline foundation arc. After this lands: foundation 100% complete. Rule pipeline canonical, populated, and self-governing. Three instances (#3, #4, #5) credit EB-agent contribution via rationale and notes fields — proving the cross-chat relay pattern works for governance content.
